expectations.text: Output contains both a recommended solution AND a distinct runner-up alternative
expectations.evidence: output.md: '## RECOMMENDED → Sliding Window Counter + Adaptive Throttling Hybrid (B')' and '## ALTERNATIVE → Token Bucket + Lua Atomicity Stack (A')' — two fully elaborated distinct solutions.

expectations.text: Evidence of 3 divergent candidates generated in Phase 1 (A/B/C labeling or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: output.md TRACE section: 'A: Token Bucket + Lua Atomicity Stack, B: Sliding Window Counter + Adaptive Throttling, C: Policy-as-Configuration Gateway Pattern (Kong)'. Divergence scores: A vs B ~7/10, A vs C ~9/10, B vs C ~9/10.

expectations.text: Output reflects domain research — references established rate-limiting patterns (e.g., token bucket, sliding window, leaky bucket, fixed window) rather than only generic advice
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 produced 10-requirement enriched registry. Output includes: token bucket, sliding window counter, boundary-burst analysis, RFC 6585, IETF draft-ietf-httpapi-ratelimit-headers, Redis Lua atomicity, XFF right-to-left walk, circuit breaker patterns, Condorcet tally. Deep domain specificity throughout.

expectations.text: Winner recommendation includes concrete implementation guidance beyond just naming a pattern
expectations.evidence: output.md includes: complete Lua script, Python key construction with hash tags, header-building code, graduated warning logic, per-endpoint-type fail policy code, retry storm detection code, trusted proxy XFF walk code, and observability metric definitions.
